content.problemStatement: Write a C program to find the sum of two given numbers, 4 and 8. The program should compute the sum and display the result as output.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains no input.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the sum of 4 and 8 on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The program prints the sum of 4 and 8 for the only test case.
The program prints the sum of 4 and 8 for each of the two test cases.
The program prints the sum of 4 and 8 for each of the three test cases.

solution.approach: The problem requires computing the sum of two fixed numbers, 4 and 8. Since the numbers are fixed and do not depend on any input, the solution simply prints the sum of these two numbers for each test case. The time complexity is O(1) per test case because it involves a constant number of operations, and the space complexity is also O(1) as it uses a constant amount of space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) per test case, O(T) total.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) — The dominant structure is a constant.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(sys.stdin.readline())
for _ in range(T):
    print(12)
